title: sphaeralcea brevipes
intro: Sphaeralcea brevipes, also known as the desert marigold or orange globe mallow, is a perennial wildflower native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be used to treat various health issues such as wounds, fever, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Sphaeralcea brevipes include its ability to reduce inflammation, fight off infections, and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, allowing for the extraction and utilization of its medicinal properties.
